title: Proof Techniques, Invariants, and Recurrence Bounds · Adversarial-Input Lab
summary: Review a proposed optimization whose runtime and correctness claims lack evidence. The scenario, data, constraints, and failure modes differ from the workshop. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of a formal algorithm review packet with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use induction on input size or recursive structure to prove algorithm properties.
State initialization, maintenance, and termination for nontrivial loop invariants.
Use contradiction and exchange arguments where direct construction is awkward.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
